Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Boomloom: Think with your hands 145 upvotes, 15 comments
  2. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 51 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. My first patch to the Linux kernel 147 upvotes, 25 comments
  4. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 221 upvotes, 47 comments
  5. Some things just take time 763 upvotes, 244 comments
  6. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 229 upvotes, 66 comments
  7. Bayesian statistics for confused data scientists 150 upvotes, 41 comments
  8. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 307 upvotes, 111 comments
  9. A Review of Dice That Came with the White Castle 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 114 upvotes, 36 comments
  11. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 62 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. 25 Years of Eggs 109 upvotes, 37 comments
  13. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. Turns out your coffee addiction may be doing your brain a favor 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. 'Miracle': Europe reconnects with lost spacecraft 59 upvotes, 24 comments
  16. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 531 upvotes, 304 comments
  17. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 379 upvotes, 219 comments
  18. Hormuz Minesweeper – Are you tired of winning? 412 upvotes, 248 comments
  19. Chest Fridge (2009) 140 upvotes, 77 comments
  20. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 25 upvotes, 9 comments
  21. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Can Programming Be Liberated from the von Neumann Style? (1977) [pdf] 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Vatican Rebukes Peter Thiel's Antichrist Lectures in Rome 68 upvotes, 42 comments
  24. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 21 upvotes, 9 comments
  25. Revise – An AI Editor for Documents 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  26. Windows native app development is a mess 59 upvotes, 59 comments
  27. A Case Against Currying 10 upvotes, 9 comments
  28. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 6 upvotes, 4 comments
  29. Convincing Is Not Persuading 5 upvotes, 3 comments
  30. $ teebot.dev – from terminal to tee in 6 seconds 6 upvotes, 6 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 22 March 2026. Source here.
